description:
Advances: specled.implementation_tier

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Realization.Implementation orchestrator; SpecLedEx.Realization.Closure.compute/2 walks MFA callee edges from the tracer side-manifest starting at declared implementation bindings, stopping at subject boundaries + out-of-project modules + already-visited MFAs; Closure.subject_for_mfa/2 with MFA ownership rule (realized_by.implementation wins; surface-file mapping with lexical tiebreak; otherwise shared helper); hash-ref composition (Cargo pattern) — closures reference other subjects' hashes by id + hash string, not canonical AST; shared-helper accounting inlines orphan MFAs into each caller's closure; Scenario 1 integration test (scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs
  - mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.closure_walk_stops_at_boundary
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.shared_helper_inlined
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.ownership_lexical_tiebreak
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.refactor_does_not_drift (Scenario 1)

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs
- test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/** (S3a owns tracer + xref)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (extended in S5b only)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex (S5a)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex (S5b)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT introduce a configurable closure_strategy (scope cut #1) — hardcode subject_boundary.
- Do NOT define Coverage.subject_for_mfa/1 here (scope audit #17); Closure owns ownership.
- Do NOT persist closure sets in state.json; recompute per-check.
- Hash-ref composition uses the string form "subject:<id>:hash:<hash>" in the hash input, not the referenced canonical AST.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check passes on specled_ex's own repo
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: UUID not available in this harness
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/<session-uuid>.jsonl

Commit: 4b43d32eb5090c32d6def8109267eb38a778c8fb
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (new)
- test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs (new)
- test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md (stubs flipped to execute: true)
- .spec/state.json (index recomputed by mix spec.check)

Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.Realization.ClosureTest (8 tests, covers: closure_walks_tracer_edges, ownership_rule, shared_helper_accounting)
- SpecLedEx.Realization.ImplementationTest (9 tests, covers: hash_ref_composition, closure_walks_tracer_edges, dangling + drift)
- SpecLedEx.Integration.ScenarioRefactorStableTest (1 integration test, covers: scenario_refactor_stable — the gating criterion of Scenario 1)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs → execute: true
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → execute: true
- mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration → execute: true

Summary: Closure.compute/2 walks the tracer MFA edge manifest from a subject's declared implementation bindings, stopping at subject boundaries (ownership via realized_by.implementation first, then spec-meta.surface file-match with lexical tiebreak), out-of-project modules, and already-visited MFAs; orphans inline as shared helpers. Implementation.run/4 composes per-subject impl hashes with Cargo-style hash references ("subject:<id>:hash:<hex>") to other subjects — A's hash flips when B's impl hash flips, without inlining B's canonical AST. A deep_strip_meta pass inside the orchestrator (kept local to Implementation; Canonical module was not in Allowed touches) handles the line-meta on dot-expression form tuples that Canonical.strip_meta's shallow recursion misses — this is the mechanical enabler for the refactor_does_not_drift scenario.

Test results:
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs → 8 pass
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → 9 pass
- mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration → 1 pass
- Full suite: mix test --include integration → 313 tests, 0 failures
- mix spec.check → 20 pre-existing warnings against other still-draft subjects (coverage_capture, expanded_behavior_tier, triangulation, use_tier — S4/S5 work); ZERO findings against specled.implementation_tier. Prior ticket (q59.3) accepted the same pre-existing state.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: UUID not available to subagent
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/<session-uuid>.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7
- Files outside Allowed globs: 2 — both are spec-feedback-loop allowances accepted by prior tickets (q59.2/q59.3):
    - .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md (the subject's own spec, verification stubs flipped to execute: true)
    - .spec/state.json (recomputed by mix spec.check; diff shows only removal of the 4 prior surface_target_missing warnings that implementation.ex/closure.ex/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs/closure_test.exs used to produce)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0. Confirmed: no lib/specled_ex/compiler/**, no api_boundary.ex, no drift.ex, no expanded_behavior.ex, no use.ex, no coverage/**, no coverage_triangulation.ex.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3, passed: 3
    - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs -> 8 tests, 0 failures
    - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs -> 9 tests, 0 failures
    - mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration -> 1 test, 0 failures
- Requirements with passing tests: 5/5
    - closure_walks_tracer_edges: closure_test.exs exercises all three stop conditions — subject boundary ("walk stops at subject boundary..." at L24 refutes Mod.B.bar/0 and Mod.B.helper/0 in A's owned set), out-of-project ("callees in non-project modules are dropped from the walk" at L102 with in_project? closure), cycle ("already-visited MFAs do not cause infinite recursion" at L82 with mutual recursion).
    - ownership_rule: closure_test.exs L120 "binding match wins over surface" (bbb has the surface match, aaa wins via binding) + L128 "with no binding, surface match with lexical tiebreak picks aaa over bbb" (both have identical surface, aaa wins lexicographically).
    - shared_helper_accounting: closure_test.exs L46 demonstrates the orphan Helpers.util/0 is inlined; implementation_test.exs L185 "shared helper's AST is inlined — its body changes ripple into the caller's hash" recompiles Helpers.util/1 with a different body and asserts the Solo hash flips. Body-affects-caller is directly exercised.
    - hash_ref_composition: two-part evidence. (a) closure_test.exs L24 refutes B's MFAs in A's owned_mfas, so B's AST cannot enter A's hash input by construction; implementation.ex L179-185 constructs hash_input from owned_asts/shared_asts/ref_tuples (the latter are "subject:<id>:hash:<hex>" strings, L263). (b) implementation_test.exs L156 "A's hash changes when B's referenced hash marker changes" mutates B's edges, A's owned MFAs unchanged, A's hash flips — proving dependency flows through the ref string.
    - scenario_refactor_stable: scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs compiles @main_src, commits the hash via HashStore.write, recompiles @refactored_src (renamed locals input/result/value/next/count, reflowed bodies), runs Implementation.run, and asserts no branch_guard_realization_drift finding.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
    - No closure_strategy option anywhere (grep clean across both lib files).
    - No Coverage.subject_for_mfa/1 (grep clean; ownership lives on Closure.subject_for_mfa/2 as intended).
    - No closure set persistence in state.json (diff shows only the 4 removed surface_target_missing warnings; closures are recomputed per-check in Implementation.compute_hash/4 with a per-run memo keyed by subject id).
    - No new external deps (mix.exs untouched, 0 lines in diff).
    - Cross-subject hash composition uses the exact string form "subject:<id>:hash:<hex>" at implementation.ex:263 (also :266 unresolved / :270 unknown fallbacks for missing refs — same shape).
- Project verification: PASS for specled.implementation_tier specifically. mix spec.check output grepped for "implementation_tier" returns 0 lines. The 20 pre-existing warnings are all against coverage_capture (6), expanded_behavior_tier (6), triangulation (5), use_tier (3) — matching the exact pre-existing state q59.3 explicitly accepted.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The implementer added a local deep_strip_meta/1 inside Implementation (L376-392) to strip line-meta on remote-call dot-expression form tuples that Canonical.strip_meta's shallow recursion misses. This is necessary for scenario.refactor_does_not_drift to pass (cosmetic edits shift line numbers on remote calls). Keeping it local to Implementation avoids touching lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex which is not in Allowed; this is the correct scope-respecting workaround. A future ticket could consolidate into Canonical.
- implementation_test.exs setup_all compiles ephemeral fixtures into System.tmp_dir!() and :code.load_file's them; this is a test-harness pattern, not a new dep, and does not interact with the project's own compile tracer.
- The closure cycle guard test (closure_test.exs:82) happens to also cover another case — when a subject has multiple implementation bindings joined by a mutual-recursion edge. The assertion is minimal but sufficient: the test terminates, which would fail with a StackOverflow absent the visited set.

title: S3b: implementation tier + subject-boundary closure
